claims.text: Label projection can extend span-annotated datasets from high-resource languages to low-resource languages
claims.source_quote: Label projection is an effective technique for cross-lingual transfer, extending span-annotated datasets from a high-resource language to low-resource ones.

claims.text: Prior approaches perform label projection as a separate step after machine translation
claims.source_quote: Most approaches perform label projection as a separate step after machine translation

claims.text: Previous work combining translation and label projection reported degraded translation quality
claims.source_quote: prior work that combines the two reports degraded translation quality

claims.text: LabelPigeon jointly performs translation and label projection using XML tags
claims.source_quote: We re-evaluate this claim with LabelPigeon, a novel framework that jointly performs translation and label projection via XML tags

claims.text: LabelPigeon outperforms baseline approaches in label projection
LabelPigeon actively improves translation quality in 11 languages
claims.source_quote: and actively improves translation quality in 11 languages

claims.text: The authors assessed translation quality across 203 languages with varying annotation complexity
claims.source_quote: We further assess translation quality across 203 languages and varying annotation complexity

claims.text: Additional fine-tuning consistently improved translation quality
claims.source_quote: finding consistent improvement attributed to additional fine-tuning

claims.text: The approach achieved substantial cross-lingual transfer gains across 27 languages and three downstream tasks
claims.source_quote: across 27 languages and three downstream tasks, we report substantial gains in cross-lingual transfer over comparable work, up to +39.9 F1 on NER
